The sample project demonstrates how to use StoreKit in a SwiftUI-based iOS app with a simulated app server, SKDemoServer.
To test your implementation of In-App Purchases using StoreKit, you can use StoreKit Testing in Xcode and the sandbox environment.
To test the sample in Xcode: Select the sample target, then configure it to use your Developer team for signing.
To prepare this sample code project to run in the sandbox, perform the following steps in App Store Connect: Find or create an app that supports In-App Purchases and you can use for testing purposes.
Use information from to set up the In-App Purchase.

To prepare this sample code project to use information from App Store Connect, perform the following steps in Xcode: Select the sample target, then change the bundle ID to your testing app’s bundle ID.
Configure the target to use your Developer team for signing.
Use the In-App Purchase API to give people the ability to purchase virtual goods within your app or directly from the App Store using the StoreKit framework.
First, you set up your app to register and use a single-transaction queue observer at launch.
When you receive a prompt to authenticate the purchase, use your test user account.
Be sure to keep a strong reference to the products request object; the system may release it before the request completes.
The sample app requires an iOS or iPadOS device.
Apps can also use this method to resume any activities that they pause before presenting the store.
Be aware that the system displays the review prompt to a user a maximum of three times within a 365-day period.
Avoid showing a request for a review immediately when a user launches your app, even if it isn’t the first time it launches.
Avoid requesting a review as the result of a user action.

In addition, the app and its use meets the following conditions before displaying the prompt: The app hasn’t shown a review prompt for a version of the app bundle that matches the current bundle version.
The person successfully completes the three-step process at least four times.
A person must pause on the Process Completed scene for a few seconds.

The design of this sample doesn’t require any special setup, or even the creation of products in App Store Connect; it uses StoreKit Testing in Xcode and the Transaction Manager window in Xcode to test In-App Purchase.
For more information on the product types that App Store Connect supports, see The transactions this app demonstrates don’t get information from App Store Connect and don’t incur charges.
